revision.text:
[[File:Abeno-jinja-haiden1.jpg|thumb|Abeno Shrine worship hall]]'''Abeno Shrine''' (金崎宮, ''Kanegasaki-gū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] Prefecture, Japan. Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890, and enshrines the [[kami]] of [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]].
In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''Kanpei-chūsha'').
==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]
==External links== *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gū}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1890 establishments]] [[ja:金崎宮]]

==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]
==External links== *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website]

revision.comment: expand
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | type = | dedication = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded = 1890 | closed = | founder = | priest = | reisai = | honden = | location_map = Japan | coordinates ={{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | address = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | phone = 0770-22-0938 | website = http://kanegasakigu.jp }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''Kanpei-chūsha''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:Pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] 金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] 金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = Kanegasaki-gu.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35.6645|136.0741|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]] | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] In the former [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second rank (官幣中社, ''[[Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[Prince Takanaga]] and [[Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:Kanegasaki-gū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}}

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[sn: failed]] }} [[sn:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[sn:anegasaki-gū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanegasaki-gu}}

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn: failed]] [[sn:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[sn:anegasaki-gū]]

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[Meiji government]] to commemorate the events of the [[Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn: failed]] [[sn:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[sn:anegasaki-gū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[:en:Kanagasaki Castle|Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[:en:Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[:en:Nitta Yoshiaki|Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[:en:Prince Takanaga|Prince Takanaga]] and [[:en:Prince Tsunenaga|Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to commemorate the events of the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn: failed]] [[sn:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[sn:anegasaki-gū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]]

[[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = File:Kanegasaki Shrine.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.6645|N|136.074|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takayoshi-shinnō|qid=Q7244257}}; {{ill|Tsuneyoshi-shinnō|qid=Q3541669}} | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga|qid=Q847581}} | part_of = {{ill|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|qid=Q10289770}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second Rank (官幣中社, ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[:en:Kanagasaki Castle|Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[:en:Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[:en:Nitta Yoshiaki|Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[:en:Prince Takanaga|Prince Takanaga]] and [[:en:Prince Tsunenaga|Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to commemorate the events of the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11647043}}

[[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[sn:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = File:Kanegasaki Shrine.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.6645|N|136.074|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takayoshi-shinnō|WD=Q7244257}}; {{ill|Tsuneyoshi-shinnō|WD=Q3541669}} | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}} | part_of = {{ill|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q10289770}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], Japan. In the former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second Rank (官幣中社, ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of [[:en:Kanagasaki Castle|Kanagasaki Castle]], where during the [[:en:Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)]] a major battle was fought between the forces of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]] and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], led by [[:en:Nitta Yoshiaki|Nitta Yoshiaki]], the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes [[:en:Prince Takanaga|Prince Takanaga]] and [[:en:Prince Tsunenaga|Prince Tsunenaga]]. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] to commemorate the events of the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11647043}} [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[sn:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647043]] Wikidata name: Kanegasaki-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = File:Kanegasaki Shrine.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.6645|N|136.074|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takayoshi-shinnō|WD=Q7244257}}; {{ill|Tsuneyoshi-shinnō|WD=Q3541669}} | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581}} | part_of = {{ill|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q10289770}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, Japan. In the former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second Rank (官幣中社, ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of {{ill|Kanagasaki Castle|en|Kanagasaki Castle|WD=Q11646283|lt=Kanagasaki Castle}}, where during the {{ill|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|en|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|WD=Q7510148|lt=Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)}} a major battle was fought between the forces of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}} and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, led by {{ill|Nitta Yoshiaki|en|Nitta Yoshiaki|WD=Q3342237|lt=Nitta Yoshiaki}}, the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes {{ill|Prince Takanaga|en|Prince Takanaga|WD=Q7244257|lt=Prince Takanaga}} and {{ill|Prince Tsunenaga|en|Prince Tsunenaga|WD=Q3541669|lt=Prince Tsunenaga}}. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}} to commemorate the events of the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11647043}} [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[co:commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]][[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Kanegasaki-gu]][[en:Kanegasaki-gū]][[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]][[ja:金崎宮]] {{Short description|Shrine in Tsuruga, Fukui Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kanegasaki Shrine
金崎宮 | image = File:Kanegasaki Shrine.jpg | caption = Kanegasaki-gū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.6645|N|136.074|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takayoshi-shinnō|WD=Q7244257|1=Prince Takanaga}}; {{ill|Tsuneyoshi-shinnō|WD=Q3541669|1=Prince Tsunenaga}} | founded_by = | established = 1890 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-4 Kanegasaki-cho, Tsuruga-shi, Fukui-ken 914-0072 Japan | website = {{URL|http://kanegasakigu.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga|WD=Q847581|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}} | part_of = {{ill|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q10289770|1=List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kanegasaki Shrine''' |金崎宮|Kanegasaki-gū}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, Japan. In the former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was an imperial shrine of the second Rank (官幣中社, ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''). Its main festival is held annually on May 6. It was founded in 1890 on the site of {{ill|Kanagasaki Castle|en|Kanagasaki Castle|WD=Q11646283|lt=Kanagasaki Castle|1=Kanagasaki Castle}}, where during the {{ill|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|en|Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|WD=Q7510148|lt=Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)|1=Siege of Kanegasaki (1337)}} a major battle was fought between the forces of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} and forces loyal to the [[Southern Court]] of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, led by {{ill|Nitta Yoshiaki|en|Nitta Yoshiaki|WD=Q3342237|lt=Nitta Yoshiaki|1=Nitta Yoshiaki}}, the son of [[Nitta Yoshisada]] and the imperial princes {{ill|Prince Takanaga|en|Prince Takanaga|WD=Q7244257|lt=Prince Takanaga|1=Prince Takanaga}} and {{ill|Prince Tsunenaga|en|Prince Tsunenaga|WD=Q3541669|lt=Prince Tsunenaga|1=Prince Tsunenaga}}. The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} to commemorate the events of the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11647043}} [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

The forces of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern Court]] prevailed, and Nitta Yoshiaki and Prince Takanaga took their own lives to avoid the disgrace of capture, whereas Prince Tsunenaga managed to escape but was captured and killed shortly afterwards.{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334-1615 |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |ISBN=0804705259 |pages=63–64}} It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} to commemorate the events of the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== ==External links== {{commonscat|Kanegasaki-gu}} *[http://kanegasakigu.jp Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[arz:كانيجاساكى جو]] {{Commons category|Kanegasaki-gu}} [[fr:Kanegasaki-gū]] [[ja:金崎宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11647043}} [[Category:1890 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
